papers.title: AbdomenGen: Sequential Volume-Conditioned Diffusion Framework for Abdominal Anatomy Generation
papers.abstract: AbdomenGen is a sequential, volume-conditioned diffusion framework designed for generating controllable abdominal anatomy computational phantoms. Its core contribution is the **Volume Control Scalar (VCS)**, which standardizes and decouples organ size modulation from overall body habitus. This allows for interpretable, independent control over multiple organ volumes while maintaining global anatomical coherence during sequential mask synthesis.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of AbdomenGen, sequential organ generation framework based on denoising diffusion U-Net with FiLM conditioning. For generation of organ o i o_{i} (illustrated left to right for colon), the model p θ i p_{\theta_{i}} takes as input the body mask signed distance field (SDF) B B , current context SDF S i ctx S_{i}^{\mathrm{ctx}} , and volume control scalar v o v_{o} . The scalar is injected through a FiLM adapter to modulate intermediate network features. The generated organ SDF S ^ o i \hat{S}_{o_{i}} is obtained and incorporated into the context to form S i + 1 ctx S_{i+1}^{\mathrm{ctx}} . Organs are generated in a predefined order (bottom), and the accumulated outputs form the final full abdominal anatomy.

papers.title: Brain-DiT: A Universal Multi-state fMRI Foundation Model with Metadata-Conditioned Pretraining
papers.abstract: Brain-DiT is a universal multi-state fMRI foundation model pretrained on a massive and diverse dataset spanning various brain states. Its core method involves using metadata-conditioned diffusion pretraining with a Diffusion Transformer (DiT), allowing it to learn robust, multi-scale representations. This approach is shown to be a superior pretraining strategy compared to traditional reconstruction or alignment methods for generalizing across diverse fMRI data.
papers.figure_caption: Brain-DiT framework: pretraining and downstream adaptation. (a) Pretraining. An ROI time-series window is noised at a random timestep t t and denoised by a conditional/unconditional DiT using v v -prediction. (b) Downstream adaptation. Multiple noisy views at selected timesteps are processed by the shared pretrained DiT. Token features from selected layers are aggregated to form a representation for downstream classification or regression.

papers.title: Domain-Specific Latent Representations Improve the Fidelity of Diffusion-Based Medical Image Super-Resolution
papers.abstract: This paper demonstrates that the domain-agnostic Variational Autoencoder (VAE) component in latent diffusion models is the primary bottleneck for medical image super-resolution quality. The core contribution is replacing the standard VAE with a domain-specific autoencoder, MedVAE, pretrained on extensive medical data. This substitution significantly improves reconstruction fidelity, yielding substantial PSNR gains across various modalities by better encoding anatomically relevant fine structures.
papers.figure_caption: Latent diffusion SR pipeline. Both methods share an identical LDM UNet (purple); only the VAE encoder/decoder differs. Top: SD-VAE SR uses a generic VAE trained on natural images (4-channel, 32 × 32 32\times 32 latent). Bottom: MedVAE SR (proposed) uses a domain-specific VAE trained on medical images (3-channel, 64 × 64 64\times 64 latent). The controlled substitution isolates the VAE’s contribution to SR fidelity.

papers.title: Fundus Image-based Glaucoma Screening via Retinal Knowledge-Oriented Dynamic Multi-Level Feature Integration
papers.abstract: This paper proposes a novel framework for glaucoma screening using fundus images that explicitly integrates retinal anatomical knowledge. It employs a tri-branch structure to capture global context, optic disc/cup structure, and dynamically localized pathological regions. The core contribution is the **retinal knowledge-oriented dynamic multi-level feature integration**, which uses a Dynamic Window Mechanism to adaptively focus on diagnostically relevant areas, improving robustness over purely data-driven methods.
papers.figure_caption: Conceptual illustration of the proposed retinal knowledge-oriented glaucoma screening framework. Existing deep learning models rely primarily on purely data-driven attention mechanisms, which suffer from limited anatomical awareness, structural uncertainty in optic disc–cup regions, and poor generalization across heterogeneous imaging devices. To address these challenges, the proposed framework integrates retinal priors extracted from the RetFound foundation model to guide attention learning. Knowledge-guided attention enables the model to jointly capture global retinal context, structural features of the optic disc–cup complex, and dynamically localized pathological regions. By combining retinal knowledge injection with multi-scale feature integration, the proposed framework achieves robust and interpretable glaucoma screening across diverse clinical datasets.

papers.title: GeoAlign: Geometric Feature Realignment for MLLM Spatial Reasoning
papers.abstract: GeoAlign addresses the spatial reasoning limitations in MLLMs by proposing a dynamic feature aggregation method. It overcomes the misalignment bias of static 3D feature extraction by constructing a hierarchical geometric feature bank. The MLLM's visual tokens act as content-aware queries to perform layer-wise sparse routing, adaptively selecting the most relevant multi-layer geometric features for improved spatial understanding.
papers.figure_caption: Task misalignment bias. Feature evolution progressively aligns with the pretraining tasks, thus many geometric features valuable for spatial reasoning tasks are distributed in the preceding layers.

papers.title: HTDC: Hesitation-Triggered Differential Calibration for Mitigating Hallucination in Large Vision-Language Models
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **Hesitation-Triggered Differential Calibration (HTDC)**, a training-free method to reduce hallucination in LVLMs. HTDC identifies grounding instability via "layer-wise hesitation," a signal of fluctuating token preference across intermediate layers. Calibration is then selectively triggered only at these hesitation-prone steps, using lightweight visual and semantic nullification probes to suppress hallucination without constant, costly intervention.

papers.title: MODIX: A Training-Free Multimodal Information-Driven Positional Index Scaling for Vision-Language Models
papers.abstract: MODIX is a training-free framework that addresses suboptimal positional encoding in Vision-Language Models by dynamically scaling positional indices. It achieves this by jointly modeling intra-modal information density (via entropy) and inter-modal interaction (via cross-modal alignment) to derive unified scores. This allows MODIX to allocate finer positional granularity to more informative content and compress redundant regions without altering the model's parameters.
papers.figure_caption: MODIX framework. Dual pathways analyze multimodal embeddings 𝐄 \mathbf{E} to compute information contributions C ~ m \tilde{C}_{m} , which determine adaptive vision strides while text maintains unit stride. Adjusted indices 𝐏 ′ \mathbf{P}^{\prime} integrate directly into RoPE without parameter updates.

papers.title: PR-MaGIC: Prompt Refinement Via Mask Decoder Gradient Flow For In-Context Segmentation
papers.abstract: PR-MaGIC is a training-free, test-time framework that refines prompts for in-context segmentation models like SAM. It leverages the gradient flow from SAM's mask decoder to iteratively adjust the initial prompts, addressing visual inconsistencies between support and query images. This method significantly improves segmentation quality without requiring additional model training.
papers.figure_caption: Illustration of PR-MaGIC. PR-MaGIC iteratively refines prompts for segmentation by updating the embedding vector distribution ρ t \rho_{t} with mask decoder gradient flow. This process minimizes the KL divergence between ρ t \rho_{t} and the target embedding vector distribution μ \mu from the ground truth mask. At each iteration t t , given an initial set of prompts and their corresponding segmentation masks, the embedding vector z t z_{t} is updated along the gradient flow derived from the mask decoder, which in turn updates ρ t \rho_{t} .

papers.title: Representation geometry shapes task performance in vision-language modeling for CT enterography
papers.abstract: This paper investigates the optimal representation geometry for vision-language models applied to CT enterography for Inflammatory Bowel Disease assessment. The core finding is that **mean pooling** excels at categorical disease classification, while **attention pooling** is superior for cross-modal retrieval, indicating different aggregation strategies emphasize distinct representational properties. Furthermore, encoding **tissue contrast via multi-window RGB mapping** is more critical for performance than increasing spatial coverage through multiplanar sampling.

papers.title: RSGMamba: Reliability-Aware Self-Gated State Space Model for Multimodal Semantic Segmentation
papers.abstract: This paper introduces RSGMamba, a novel framework for multimodal semantic segmentation that addresses the issue of unequal modality reliability. Its core component, the Reliability-aware Self-Gated Mamba Block (RSGMB), explicitly models the reliability of each modality and uses a self-gating mechanism to dynamically regulate cross-modal interactions. This allows the model to perform reliability-aware feature selection, preventing noisy or incomplete auxiliary modalities from degrading the final segmentation results.

papers.title: Scaling Exposes the Trigger: Input-Level Backdoor Detection in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models via Cross-Attention Scaling
papers.abstract: This paper proposes a novel, input-level backdoor detection method for Text-to-Image diffusion models based on actively probing the model's internal state. The core method involves applying controlled scaling perturbations to the cross-attention mechanism and observing the resulting divergence in response patterns between benign and backdoor inputs, termed Cross-Attention Scaling Response Divergence (CSRD). This technique offers a more stable and trigger-agnostic detection approach compared to relying on observable input anomalies.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. Robustness to stealthy and explicit triggers. Dark bars denote the AUROC under the stealthy IBA attack, while light bars represent the deviation from the mean AUROC across four explicit-trigger attacks.

papers.title: Why and When Visual Token Pruning Fails? A Study on Relevant Visual Information Shift in MLLMs Decoding
papers.abstract: This paper investigates why visual token pruning fails in complex reasoning tasks for Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), identifying the **Relevant Visual Information Shift (RVIS)** during decoding as the core issue. The authors propose **Decoding-stage Shift-aware Token Pruning (DSTP)**, a training-free framework that aligns pruned visual tokens with the dynamic reasoning requirements of the decoding process. DSTP effectively mitigates performance drops in complex reasoning while maintaining or improving performance on simpler visual understanding tasks.
papers.figure_caption: (a) Performance retention rates of various token pruning methods on Qwen3-VL [ qwen3vl ] and InternVL3.5 [ internvl3_5 ] across VQA and VMR. (b)–(f) Attention heatmaps for a MathVerse [ mathverse ] sample on Qwen3-VL relative to the text token whose attention shifts drastically, reflecting the reasoning context. 3 3 3 Regarding how the specific text token for the attention heatmap is selected, please refer to Sec. ˜ 3 . Full sentences are provided to offer complete context for each reasoning step.

papers.title: Agentic Discovery with Active Hypothesis Exploration for Visual Recognition
papers.abstract: HypoExplore is an agentic framework that frames neural architecture discovery as a hypothesis-driven scientific inquiry, guided by a large language model. It iteratively ideates, implements, and refines architectures using evolutionary branching, balancing exploration and exploitation based on confidence scores tracked in a memory bank updated by multiple feedback agents. The core contribution is this structured, hypothesis-driven approach to automated visual recognition architecture search.
papers.figure_caption: High-level Overview of HypoExplore. Starting from a research direction, HypoExplore initializes a discovery state with a Trajectory Tree Memory and Hypothesis Memory Bank (Step 0 → \rightarrow Step 1). At each subsequent step, the current discovery state selects a parent node and hypothesis to guide the Research Cycle, producing an updated discovery state with enriched memory (Step t → \rightarrow Step t+1).

papers.title: All in One: A Unified Synthetic Data Pipeline for Multimodal Video Understanding
papers.abstract: This paper introduces "All in One," a unified synthetic data generation pipeline to overcome the limitations of real-world video data collection for training multimodal large language models. The core method automatically produces unlimited, richly supervised multimodal video data across various tasks (e.g., counting, QA, segmentation) within a single framework. A key contribution is the VQA-based fine-tuning strategy, which encourages deeper visual grounding and reasoning by training models on structured questions about the visual content.
papers.figure_caption: Comparison of real and synthetic data for training video foundation models. Real data (left) requires manual annotation, making it costly and labor-intensive, while synthetic data (right) is automatically generated, offering a scalable and efficient alternative for creating high-quality training datasets.

papers.title: ArtifactWorld: Scaling 3D Gaussian Splatting Artifact Restoration via Video Generation Models
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **ArtifactWorld**, a framework designed to restore artifacts in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) renderings, which often suffer from degradation under sparse views. The core method involves systematically expanding training data by creating a large dataset (107.5K clips) based on a detailed artifact taxonomy, and employing a **homogeneous dual-model paradigm** within a video diffusion backbone. This approach significantly improves temporal coherence and spatial accuracy, leading to robust restoration and better generalization across diverse 3DGS artifacts.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. ArtifactWorld effectively resolves complex 3D Gaussian Splatting degradations under sparse-view constraints. We systematically categorize these failures into nine fine-grained phenomenological artifact types (Sec. 3 ). Compared to recent generative restoration pipelines (e.g., Difix3D (Wu et al . , 2025b ) , GSFixer (Yin et al . , 2025a ) ), our large-scale data scaling and homogeneous video diffusion framework ensure spatio-temporally consistent, high-fidelity restoration.

papers.title: Boosting Robust AIGI Detection with LoRA-based Pairwise Training
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the LoRA-based Pairwise Training (LPT) strategy to enhance the robustness of AI-Generated Image (AIGI) detectors against severe, real-world distortions. The core method involves targeted finetuning of a visual foundation model using LoRA, coupled with a unique pairwise training process that simulates complex distortions and size variations seen in validation and test sets. This approach significantly boosts detection performance "in the wild" where standard detectors fail.
papers.figure_caption: Previous AIGI detection methods mainly consider the generalization performance under different generation models. However, when the images are distorted due to the transmission, the detectors will make false judgments. To make the detector practical in the downstream scenario, it is urgent to consider a robust and generalized detector.

papers.title: Boosting Visual Instruction Tuning with Self-Supervised Guidance
papers.abstract: This paper proposes **Boosting Visual Instruction Tuning (B-VIT)**, a lightweight method to improve MLLMs' fine-grained visual reasoning. It achieves this by augmenting standard instruction tuning with a small set of visually grounded self-supervised tasks, reformulated as natural language instruction triplets (e.g., rotation prediction). This forces the model to utilize visual evidence rather than relying solely on language priors, requiring no new architecture or human labels.
papers.figure_caption: V isually G rounded I nstruction F ine- T uning V-GIFT . We enhance visual instruction tuning by injecting visually grounded self-supervised tasks as additional instruction-following examples sampled from the instruction-tuning data (left; rotation prediction shown). This simple modification encourages better use of visual information and yields consistent gains on vision-centric benchmarks (right; CVB-2D, POPE, MMStar, BLINK) across model variants.

papers.title: CBAM-Enhanced DenseNet121 for Multi-Class Chest X-Ray Classification with Grad-CAM Explainability
papers.abstract: This paper proposes CBAM-DenseNet121, a transfer learning framework that integrates the Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) into DenseNet121 for the clinically relevant three-class classification of chest X-rays (Normal, Bacterial, Viral Pneumonia). The core contribution is improving diagnostic accuracy by focusing attention on relevant features while also providing Grad-CAM explainability. Additionally, the study reports a negative finding showing that EfficientNetB3 underperformed simpler baseline models for this specific task.

papers.title: Chain-of-Models Pre-Training: Rethinking Training Acceleration of Vision Foundation Models
papers.abstract: Chain-of-Models Pre-Training (CoM-PT) accelerates vision foundation model training at the model family level by establishing a sequential "model chain" ordered by size. Only the smallest model is fully pre-trained; larger models are then efficiently trained via inverse knowledge transfer from their smaller predecessors, reusing knowledge in both parameter and feature spaces. This novel method achieves performance comparable or superior to standard individual training while significantly reducing overall training time across the model family.
papers.figure_caption: Standard individual pre-training vs . Chain-of-Models Pre-Training . All ViTs [ 17 ] are trained on a combined dataset of CC3M [ 59 ] and CC12M [ 4 ] , and evaluated on ImageNet-1K [ 14 ] .

papers.title: Challenging Vision-Language Models with Physically Deployable Multimodal Semantic Lighting Attacks
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **Multimodal Semantic Lighting Attacks (MSLA)**, the first framework for physically deployable adversarial attacks against Vision-Language Models (VLMs). MSLA utilizes controllable adversarial lighting to disrupt the VLM's multimodal semantic understanding, addressing the critical gap between digital adversarial studies and real-world security risks. The core contribution is demonstrating that physical light perturbations can induce recognition failures and semantic misinterpretations in deployed VLMs.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. Overall framework of MSLA. Given a clean image, we parameterize the triangular light by its relative position, radius, color, and three polar angles, and optimize these variables with a genetic algorithm. The optimized triangular light can be deployed using simple optical devices such as a flashlight, colored transparent sheets, and shape templates.

papers.title: CLASP: Class-Adaptive Layer Fusion and Dual-Stage Pruning for Multimodal Large Language Models
papers.abstract: CLASP is a plug-and-play framework that reduces computational overhead in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) by adaptively managing visual tokens. It achieves this through **class-adaptive layer fusion** to create category-specific representations, followed by **dual-stage pruning** that allocates token budgets based on attention saliency and redundancy. This approach enables robust, aggressive visual token reduction tailored to specific input classes.
papers.figure_caption: Impact of hyperparameter settings on MMVet dataset performance (LLaVA-v1.5-7B, 192 retained tokens). The heatmaps illustrate the score distribution across different question categories under varying conditions. Top: Evaluation of five representative layer-fusion strategies (A–E), ordered by an increasing proportion of weights assigned to deeper layers ( i.e. , shifting from shallow in A to deep in E). Bottom: Sweeping the attention–similarity mixing coefficient (higher: more attention; lower: more similarity).

papers.title: Combating Pattern and Content Bias: Adversarial Feature Learning for Generalized AI-Generated Image Detection
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the Multi-dimensional Adversarial Feature Learning (MAFL) framework to improve the generalization of AI-generated image detection by combating data bias. MAFL uses a pretrained encoder and an adversarial bias-learning branch with a multi-dimensional adversarial loss. This method forces the model to learn features common across different generative models, rather than overfitting to specific patterns present in the training data.
papers.figure_caption: (Color best viewed.) This illustration shows the problems of traditional methods under the asymmetric bias learning phenomenon and our proposed solution. (a) Traditional detectors (e.g., CLIP + classifier) tend to over-rely on the specific generative patterns of particular generative models when performing real/fake classification. (b) Such reliance on specific generative patterns makes the model prone to generalization failure when encountering unseen generative patterns. (c)–(e) MAFL introduces an adversarial mechanism between the real/fake classification task and the specific-pattern classification task, guiding the model to extract common generative features that characterize different generative models. These features are free from model-specific characteristics, thereby enabling stronger generalization when facing unseen generative models.

papers.title: Decoding by Perturbation: Mitigating MLLM Hallucinations via Dynamic Textual Perturbation
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **Decoding by Perturbation (DeP)**, a training-free framework to mitigate Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) hallucinations caused by language priors overriding visual evidence. DeP works by applying controlled, dynamic textual perturbations during decoding to probe and suppress the influence of these latent language priors, using attention variance to stabilize genuine visual evidence. This method effectively mitigates hallucinations without altering visual representations or compromising the model's generative fluency.
papers.figure_caption: Illustration of multimodal hallucination driven by text hypersensitivity. Although the modified prompts preserve the core semantics of the original query, minor variations in their surface structures lead to drastically different hallucinated outputs (e.g., “fake” or “red”). As revealed by the attention maps on the right, these textual perturbations cause a severe drift in visual grounding. Notably, the generation of “red” indicates that the model has established a spurious correlation, erroneously shifting its visual attention (bottom map) to the red hoodie rather than grounding it on the glasses.

papers.title: Don't Show Pixels, Show Cues: Unlocking Visual Tool Reasoning in Language Models via Perception Programs
papers.abstract: This paper argues that feeding raw, pixel-level outputs from vision tools hinders multimodal language models (MLLMs) because these representations conflict with the LLMs' language-native strengths. The core contribution is **Perception Programs ($\text{P}^2$)**, a training-free method that transforms dense tool outputs into compact, structured, language-native summaries. This approach significantly improves MLLMs' visual reasoning by providing cues that the models can directly parse and utilize.

papers.title: From Attenuation to Attention: Variational Information Flow Manipulation for Fine-Grained Visual Perception
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the **Variational Information Flow (VIF)** framework to combat **Visual Attenuation**, where fine-grained visual details are lost in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). VIF uses a Conditional Variational Autoencoder (CVAE) to model the visual saliency relevant to the query as a latent distribution. This plug-and-play module effectively manipulates information flow to preserve crucial fine-grained visual signals during deep processing.
papers.figure_caption: Illustration of Visual Attenuation. (Left) Schematic view showing visual tokens (blue) fading as they propagate through deep layers, while textual tokens (green) dominate. (Right) Quantitative layer-to-layer changes in vision attention ratio, averaged over 500 randomly sampled instances. The sharp drop in early layers indicates a premature loss of visual details.

papers.title: Generative Refinement Networks for Visual Synthesis
papers.abstract: This paper introduces Generative Refinement Networks (GRN) to overcome the inefficiency of diffusion models and the limitations of discrete tokenization in autoregressive (AR) models. GRN achieves near-lossless representation via Hierarchical Binary Quantization (HBQ) and employs a global, progressive refinement mechanism to enhance AR generation quality, similar to an artist refining a painting. This approach enables complexity-aware synthesis through an entropy-guided sampling strategy.
papers.figure_caption: Qualitative results for the class-to-image generation task.

papers.title: Habitat-GS: A High-Fidelity Navigation Simulator with Dynamic Gaussian Splatting
papers.abstract: Habitat-GS extends Habitat-Sim by integrating 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) for high-fidelity, real-time photorealistic rendering. Its core contribution is enabling the simulation of dynamic human-populated environments using "gaussian avatars," which act as both visually realistic entities and effective navigation obstacles. This significantly improves the visual fidelity and realism of embodied AI training for human-aware navigation.
papers.figure_caption: Habitat-GS is a navigation-centric embodied simulation platform with 3DGS and dynamic gaussian avatars. Compared to traditional mesh-based simulators (left), our 3DGS-based simulator (right) preserves high-frequency visual details and view-dependent effects, while gaussian avatars provide realistic and dynamic human presence for human-aware navigation scenarios, thus helping train more robust agents.

papers.title: Image-to-Image Translation Framework Embedded with Rotation Symmetry Priors
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a novel image-to-image translation (I2I) framework that incorporates **rotation symmetry priors** using **rotation group equivariant convolutions**. This method ensures the preservation of rotation symmetry, an intrinsic domain-invariant property, throughout the translation process. The core contribution is the development of a **rotation equivariant I2I framework** to enhance translation quality, particularly in unsupervised settings.
papers.figure_caption: (a) An input image for I2I containing numerous rotationally symmetric structures (illustrated using the iphone2dslr example). (b–c) Intermediate feature maps and translation results from a standard CNN, showing noticeable symmetry loss and structural artifacts. (d–e) Intermediate feature maps and translation results from the rotation equivariant network, demonstrating superior preservation of feature symmetry and producing higher-fidelity outputs.

papers.title: NTIRE 2026 The 3rd Restore Any Image Model (RAIM) Challenge: Professional Image Quality Assessment (Track 1)
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the NTIRE 2026 challenge focusing on Track 1: Professional Image Quality Assessment, addressing the limitations of traditional scalar IQA methods for high-quality images. The core method leverages Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to mimic human expert cognition by evaluating image pairs, moving beyond simple scoring to provide qualitative reasoning. The contribution is establishing a novel benchmark to test MLLMs' ability to discern subtle quality differences and articulate justifications in professional imaging contexts.
papers.figure_caption: An example of pairwise comparison under the MCQ pattern, with annotations on the rationales behind the selection of the portrait part of the curated dataset.

papers.title: Nucleus-Image: Sparse MoE for Image Generation
papers.abstract: Nucleus-Image introduces a sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) diffusion transformer for text-to-image generation, achieving state-of-the-art quality while maintaining high efficiency by activating only about 2B parameters per inference step from a total capacity of 17B. The core method utilizes Expert-Choice Routing and a streamlined architecture that omits text tokens from the main backbone. A key contribution is a decoupled routing design that stabilizes training by separating timestep-aware expert assignment from timestep-conditioned computation.
papers.figure_caption: Nucleus-Image generations of human subjects and portraits, spanning diverse cultures, ages, and artistic styles. From expressive character studies to fine-grained close-ups with intricate skin texture and detail.

papers.title: OFA-Diffusion Compression: Compressing Diffusion Model in One-Shot Manner
papers.abstract: This paper introduces OFA-Diffusion Compression, a one-shot training framework to efficiently compress Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DPMs). It addresses the need for multiple compression processes by training a single model that can yield subnetworks of various computational costs. The core contribution is restricting the candidate subnetworks to a specific set of parameter sizes to accelerate optimization compared to traditional OFA methods.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Overview of our OFA-Diffusion Compression framework. On the left, we present the structure of U-ViT architecture. On the right, we show the details of layers in blocks (FFN and Self-Attention) in our compression framework. We omit GroupNorm and time embedding for simplicity. Given a retention rate 𝒫 i \mathcal{P}_{i} , only the top- 𝒫 i \mathcal{P}_{i} important parameters will be preserved in each block, then these budgets will be unevenly allocated to each layer in a greedy manner. By doing so, for two retention rates 𝒫 i < 𝒫 j \mathcal{P}_{i}<\mathcal{P}_{j} , in each layer, the corresponded module of 𝒫 i \mathcal{P}_{i} is a subset of the module corresponded to 𝒫 j \mathcal{P}_{j} .

papers.title: Probabilistic Feature Imputation and Uncertainty-Aware Multimodal Federated Aggregation
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the Probabilistic Feature Imputation Network (P-FIN) to address missing modalities in multimodal federated learning by generating imputed features along with calibrated uncertainty estimates. This uncertainty is then used locally via sigmoid gating to weight feature reliability, and globally through Fed-UQ-Avg aggregation, improving the trustworthiness of collaborative model training in safety-critical medical settings.

papers.title: Reading Between the Pixels: Linking Text-Image Embedding Alignment to Typographic Attack Success on Vision-Language Models
papers.abstract: This paper investigates typographic prompt injection attacks on Vision-Language Models (VLMs) by rendering adversarial text as images under varying visual conditions. The core contribution is demonstrating that **text-image embedding alignment** significantly dictates attack success, showing that **mid-range font sizes** maximize ASR while very small fonts fail, suggesting defenses should focus on this alignment vulnerability across heterogeneous visual inputs.
papers.figure_caption: Example typographic prompt injection attack using the TAP jailbreak method. (a) Harmful text prompt requesting attack planning guidance. (b) Prompt rendered as a 20px typographic image. (c) 30° rotation transformation increases L2 distance from text embedding.

papers.title: Redefining Quality Criteria and Distance-Aware Score Modeling for Image Editing Assessment
papers.abstract: This paper introduces DS-IEQA, a unified framework for Image Editing Quality Assessment (IEQA) that overcomes limitations in heuristic prompting. It employs **Feedback-Driven Metric Prompt Optimization (FDMPO)** to automatically refine evaluation criteria based on probabilistic feedback. Additionally, it uses **Token-Decoupled Distance Regression Loss (TDRL)** to explicitly model the continuous structure of quality scores, improving alignment with implicit human judgment.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of the proposed Feedback-Driven Metric Prompt Optimization (FDMPO). The framework iteratively refines metric definitions via feedback signals to better align with implicit human evaluation criteria.

papers.title: ReflectCAP: Detailed Image Captioning with Reflective Memory
papers.abstract: ReflectCAP introduces a novel multi-agent pipeline that analyzes target Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) to generate "Structured Reflection Notes." These notes explicitly guide the captioning model during inference, instructing it on what factual details to avoid (hallucinations) and what fine-grained elements to attend to. This method effectively balances factuality and coverage, pushing various state-of-the-art LVLMs to the Pareto frontier on these critical captioning metrics.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of ReflectCAP. In the offline phase, a multi-agent reflective learning pipeline distills a target LVLM’s recurring captioning errors and omissions into Structured Reflection Notes. In the online phase, these notes guide caption generation for new images, producing captions that better balance factuality and coverage.

papers.title: Ride the Wave: Precision-Allocated Sparse Attention for Smooth Video Generation
papers.abstract: This paper introduces Precision-Allocated Sparse Attention (PASA) to reduce the computational cost of Video Diffusion Transformers while eliminating flickering. PASA achieves this through a training-free method that dynamically allocates computation budgets based on semantic transitions and uses hardware-aligned grouped approximations instead of global estimations. This results in highly efficient and temporally smooth high-fidelity video generation.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Visual and quantitative comparison on Wan 2.1-T2V-1.3B (quality vs. efficiency). PASA (ours) matches the end-to-end speedup of PISA ( Piecewise Sparse Attention ) while improving PSNR over other sparse attention baselines. Left: PASA preserves continuous strap geometry; other sparse methods show discontinuities. Right: PASA avoids the anatomical distortion present in competing sparse methods.

papers.title: SceneCritic: A Symbolic Evaluator for 3D Indoor Scene Synthesis
papers.abstract: SceneCritic is a novel symbolic evaluator designed to assess the spatial plausibility of 3D indoor scenes generated via intermediate representations like layouts. It leverages SceneOnto, a structured spatial ontology built from existing 3D datasets, to verify semantic, orientation, and geometric coherence across object relationships. This method provides objective, object-level assessments, overcoming the viewpoint and prompt sensitivity inherent in traditional rendering-based VLM/LLM evaluations.
papers.figure_caption: VLM Evaluation vs. SceneCritic . Given a piano room with nine objects, the VLM assigns different scores to the same scene depending on which view is rendered, while SceneCritic evaluates the layout directly by traversing relational constraints from SceneOnto (e.g., incorrect table scales, chairs not facing the table they co-occur with). This produces stable, object-level assessments that identify specific violations alongside successful placements. SceneCritic scores also align more closely with human preferences.

papers.title: T2I-BiasBench: A Multi-Metric Framework for Auditing Demographic and Cultural Bias in Text-to-Image Models
papers.abstract: T2I-BiasBench is a novel, unified evaluation framework designed to audit text-to-image models for demographic bias, element omission, and cultural collapse simultaneously, using thirteen complementary metrics. The framework evaluates models like Stable Diffusion against a baseline, integrating established measures with seven new or adapted metrics. Its core contribution is providing the first comprehensive tool to jointly assess these three critical dimensions of bias in generative diffusion models.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of the proposed Bias and Fairness Evaluation Framework for text-to-image models. The pipeline consists of prompt generation (demographic and contextual), controlled image synthesis across multiple models, attribute extraction via vision-language methods, and a multi-metric evaluation module comprising 13 fairness, diversity, and alignment metrics. The framework produces composite bias and diversity scores, enabling comparative analysis of model behavior across demographic attributes and contextual scenarios.

papers.title: Task Alignment: A simple and effective proxy for model merging in computer vision
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **Task Alignment** as a simple and effective proxy for model merging in computer vision, moving beyond restricted image classification settings. It addresses the practical challenge of hyperparameter selection when merging models with trainable, heterogeneous decoders. Task Alignment significantly speeds up this selection process by orders of magnitude while maintaining final downstream performance.
papers.figure_caption: (Left) Model merging methods produce a large number of candidates which depend on hyperparameters ( λ , μ ) (\lambda,\mu) . (Middle) SOTA methods select hyperparameters based on validation performance. This is efficient for frozen decoders like CLIP (standard benchmarks) but becomes infeasible for more complex sets of tasks that require fine-tuning multiple task-specific decoders, for each hyperparameter candidate . (Right) Our proposed Task Alignment Proxy (TAP) can be used to select hyperparameters at a fraction of the cost while maintaining performance (see also Fig. ˜ 2 ).

papers.title: Towards Realistic and Consistent Orbital Video Generation via 3D Foundation Priors
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a method for generating realistic and consistent orbital videos from a single image by leveraging 3D foundational generative models. The core contribution is using the 3D model's rich shape priors as an auxiliary constraint, specifically through global latent vectors and projected latent images, to enforce long-range structural coherence that pixel-wise attention methods fail to maintain. This approach ensures geometrically plausible and consistent video extrapolation, especially for synthesizing unseen views.

papers.title: A Sanity Check on Composed Image Retrieval
papers.abstract: This paper addresses the limitations of existing Composed Image Retrieval (CIR) benchmarks, which suffer from query ambiguity. The core contribution is the introduction of **FISD**, a new, precisely controlled benchmark that eliminates ambiguity for more accurate evaluation across six dimensions. Additionally, the authors propose an **automatic multi-round agentic evaluation framework** to better assess CIR models in sequential, interactive settings.

papers.title: ARGOS: Who, Where, and When in Agentic Multi-Camera Person Search
papers.abstract: ARGOS introduces a novel benchmark and framework that reframes multi-camera person search as an interactive reasoning task where an agent must strategically query information to locate a target. The agent uses a Spatio-Temporal Topology Graph (STTG) to plan questions and utilize spatial/temporal tools under a turn budget, addressing "Who, Where, and When." This work significantly contributes a challenging, multi-track benchmark that tests complex reasoning beyond standard perception, showing current LLMs struggle to solve these interactive search problems effectively.
papers.figure_caption: Overview. Left: An ARGOS agent interacts with an ambiguous witness through multi-turn dialogue, combining appearance, spatial, and temporal queries. Right: The four-module agent architecture (Analyst → \to Planner → \to Interviewer → \to Interpreter) forms an observe-think-act loop over the evaluation environment.

papers.title: Relaxing Anchor-Frame Dominance for Mitigating Hallucinations in Video Large Language Models
papers.abstract: This paper addresses hallucinations in Video-LLMs by identifying that they over-rely on a single "anchor frame" during generation, a bias stemming from model structure rather than video content. The core method involves mitigating this temporally imbalanced evidence aggregation by relaxing the dominance of this anchor frame during the decoding process. This decoder-side intervention aims to reduce model-specific structural bias and subsequently decrease hallucination rates.

papers.title: StructDiff: A Structure-Preserving and Spatially Controllable Diffusion Model for Single-Image Generation
papers.abstract: StructDiff is a novel single-image generation framework using a diffusion model enhanced with an **adaptive receptive field** module to preserve both global and local image structure. Its core contribution is the incorporation of **3D positional encoding** to introduce spatial priors, enabling unprecedented, flexible control over the position, scale, and fine details of the generated content.
papers.figure_caption: StructDiff enables diverse generation and controllable editing from a single training image. Top : Diverse generation at arbitrary scales. Middle : Controllable generation through PE-guided geometric editing and mask-guided fine-tuning. Bottom : Various applications without retraining. Results demonstrate effectiveness in structural preservation and spatial controllability.

papers.title: Towards Long-horizon Agentic Multimodal Search
papers.abstract: The paper introduces **LMM-Searcher**, a novel framework for long-horizon multimodal search agents that tackles context explosion and high token costs. Its core method involves **offloading visual assets to an external file system and using lightweight textual identifiers (UIDs)** to manage information efficiently. This enables progressive, on-demand visual loading, allowing the agent to maintain long-term context while preserving crucial multimodal signals.
papers.figure_caption: An illustration of LMM-Searcher. For simplicity, we employ simple strings as uids in this figure. In real implementation, we use URLs. This figure serves solely as a functional demonstration and does not represent any actual search results.

papers.title: ViLL-E: Video LLM Embeddings for Retrieval
papers.abstract: ViLL-E introduces a novel VideoLLM architecture with an adaptive embedding generation mechanism that allows for dynamic processing time based on video complexity. The core contribution is a unified model that significantly improves performance on retrieval tasks, traditionally a weakness for VideoLLMs, through a three-stage training regime combining generative and contrastive learning. This results in state-of-the-art performance across various video understanding and retrieval benchmarks.

papers.title: Visual Preference Optimization with Rubric Rewards
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **rDPO (rubric-based Direct Preference Optimization)**, a framework that leverages instance-specific, checklist-style rubrics to generate high-quality preference data for multimodal tasks. By scoring responses against these detailed criteria, rDPO creates more informative preference signals than traditional outcome-based methods. This approach significantly improves the performance of reward models and leads to superior results on downstream evaluation benchmarks.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of rDPO. The top lane constructs a model-agnostic instruction-rubric pool by mining challenging seeds via model ensembles and drafting instance-specific rubrics with structured “essential” and “additional” criteria. The bottom lane achieves on-policy data curation and optimization by sampling responses from a target policy, scoring them via rubric-grounded judging, and mining preference pairs to drive iterative DPO.

papers.title: Every Picture Tells a Dangerous Story: Memory-Augmented Multi-Agent Jailbreak Attacks on VLMs
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **MemJack**, a novel memory-augmented multi-agent framework for jailbreaking Vision-Language Models (VLMs). MemJack explicitly leverages the semantic content of natural images, coordinating multiple agents to map visual entities to malicious intents and generate effective adversarial prompts. Its core contribution is exposing the deep, semantic attack surface of VLMs beyond simple pixel or typographic manipulations.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Comparison of VLM jailbreak paradigms. Existing attacks use (a) textual manipulation, (b) visual perturbations, (c) typographic or (d) harmful images. (e) Ours MemJack exploits original natural images via multi-agent visual-semantic camouflage with memory-augmented reflection.

papers.title: QuarkMedSearch: A Long-Horizon Deep Search Agent for Exploring Medical Intelligence
papers.abstract: QuarkMedSearch is a specialized deep search agent built upon the Tongyi DeepResearch model, tailored for complex, multi-hop medical inquiries in Chinese. Its core method involves constructing long-horizon medical deep search training data by integrating a medical knowledge graph with online exploration, followed by a two-stage SFT and RL training strategy. This approach aims to significantly enhance the agent's planning, tool use, and reflection capabilities for superior performance in vertical medical intelligence exploration.
papers.figure_caption: Medical Deep Search Task Synthesis Pipeline.

papers.title: SOAR: Self-Correction for Optimal Alignment and Refinement in Diffusion Models
papers.abstract: SOAR addresses the alignment gap in diffusion model post-training by introducing a self-correction mechanism that bridges Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and Reinforcement Learning (RL). It achieves this by performing a stop-gradient rollout from a real sample, re-noising the resulting trajectory, and training the model to correct this noisy state back to the original sample. This method effectively mitigates exposure bias by enabling the model to learn from its own imperfect denoising steps, leading to improved alignment and refinement.
papers.figure_caption: Conceptual comparison of post-training regimes from a trajectory perspective. Left: SFT trains only on ground-truth denoising states. Once an inference rollout exits the narrow corridor of well-optimized states, subsequent steps sharpen the wrong structure. Middle: RL separates good and bad final outcomes with a terminal reward, but the sparse signal arrives after the erroneous rollout has already formed. Right: SOAR performs a one-step rollout to generate off-trajectory states and supervises the model to correct back toward the clean target, acting before semantic errors fully materialize.

papers.title: Coding-Free and Privacy-Preserving MCP Framework for Clinical Agentic Research Intelligence System
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the Clinical Agentic Research Intelligence System (CARIS), which automates complex clinical research workflows using LLMs orchestrated via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). The core method involves securely linking LLMs to modular tools while keeping sensitive patient data isolated on a private server. This approach enables coding-free, privacy-preserving research intelligence, allowing clinicians to execute the entire research pipeline—from planning to reporting—using only natural language commands.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of Clinical Agentic Research Intelligence System (CARIS). Given a user intent, CARIS autonomously executes a multi-step clinical research workflow by interacting with agents, heterogeneous datasets, and various tools, while allowing user intervention. Users do not have direct access to the file or DB systems.

papers.title: Development, Evaluation, and Deployment of a Multi-Agent System for Thoracic Tumor Board
papers.abstract: This paper details the development and evaluation of automated AI methods to generate succinct patient summaries for the Stanford Thoracic Tumor Board, replacing a manual process. The core contribution is the successful deployment of this multi-agent AI system into routine clinical practice, validated through comparison against physician summaries and the novel use of an LLM as a judge for fact-based scoring.

papers.title: Modality-Native Routing in Agent-to-Agent Networks: A Multimodal A2A Protocol Extension
papers.abstract: This paper introduces MMA2A, an extension to Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocols that implements **modality-native routing** by inspecting agent capabilities to forward multimodal signals (voice, image, text) in their original format. The core contribution is demonstrating that this native routing significantly boosts task accuracy (by 20 percentage points over text-bottleneck baselines), but only when the receiving agent possesses the necessary **LLM-backed reasoning capability** to exploit the preserved rich context.

papers.title: SpecBound: Adaptive Bounded Self-Speculation with Layer-wise Confidence Calibration
papers.abstract: SpecBound introduces an adaptive self-speculation method for LLMs that addresses overconfident early predictions by using layer-wise temperature annealing for early-exit decisions. It adaptively bounds speculation length based on token difficulty and efficiently reprocesses draft tokens in a unified parallel pass. This approach maximizes computational efficiency while maintaining exact output equivalence with the original model.
papers.figure_caption: Layer-wise token prediction and confidence of a segment with the MT-Bench prompt. Each cell shows the greedy-decoded token and its confidence (color intensity: darker = higher probability) at a given layer. Gray cells indicate that the token has already correctly exited at an earlier layer and is no longer considered for early-exit decisions. This figure reveals the issues of spurious high-confidence predictions and heterogeneous exit depths.

papers.title: VERITAS: Verifiable Epistemic Reasoning for Image-Derived Hypothesis Testing via Agentic Systems
papers.abstract: VERITAS is a multi-agent system designed to autonomously test natural-language hypotheses on multimodal clinical data, streamlining the fragmented process of clinical discovery. Its core contribution is providing a fully auditable evidence trail for every conclusion, tracing analysis from the initial hypothesis through code execution to the final verdict. It further introduces an epistemic evidence label framework to classify outcomes as Supported, Refuted, Underpowered, or Invalid based on significance, effect direction, and study power.
papers.figure_caption: Veritas architecture and evidence flow. Given a natural language hypothesis and dataset, three role-specialized agents (PI, Imaging Specialist, Statistician) with a Critic collaborate through four phases: (1) collaborative analysis planning producing a structured plan; (2A) neural segmentation, grounding all evidence in image-derived masks; (2B) sandboxed code generation and execution yielding statistical outputs; and (3) interpretation of the Phase 2B statistical outputs in the context of the accumulated workflow history to produce a verdict. The Evidence Classification Operator (ECO) mechanically derives the evidence label from Phase 2B outputs. Every phase produces a versioned artifact (right), forming a complete provenance trail from hypothesis to conclusion.

hacker_news.title: Turn your best AI prompts into one-click tools in Chrome
hacker_news.summary: Google is introducing "Skills in Chrome," allowing users to convert their favorite AI prompts into one-click, customizable tools directly within the Chrome browser.

hacker_news.title: Two Months After I Gave an AI $100 and No Instructions
hacker_news.summary: An experiment where an AI was given $100 and no instructions resulted in the AI autonomously generating and selling a digital product, yielding a profit.

hacker_news.title: Schools Never Taught Critical Thinking: AI Exposed the Lie
hacker_news.summary: The news story argues that the rise of AI has exposed a fundamental failure in traditional education systems, revealing that schools have largely neglected to teach genuine critical thinking skills.

hacker_news.title: AI will never be ethical or safe
hacker_news.summary: The core argument of the story is that artificial intelligence, by its very nature, can never achieve true ethical standards or guaranteed safety.

github_trending.description: A single CLAUDE.md file to improve Claude Code behavior, derived from Andrej Karpathy's observations on LLM coding pitfalls.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides a single CLAUDE.md file containing insights derived from Andrej Karpathy's observations to enhance Claude's code generation and behavior by addressing common LLM coding pitfalls.

github_trending.description: A Claude Code plugin that automatically captures everything Claude does during your coding sessions, compresses it with AI (using Claude's agent-sdk), and injects relevant context back into future sessions.
github_trending.summary: This Claude Code plugin automatically captures, AI-compresses, and injects relevant context from coding sessions back into future interactions to enhance Claude's performance.
Voicebox is an open-source voice synthesis studio that allows users to generate realistic speech, likely featuring tools for voice cloning, text-to-speech conversion, and audio manipulation.
The pascalorg/editor repository provides a tool for creating and sharing 3D architectural projects.

github_trending.description: Python tool for converting files and office documents to Markdown.
github_trending.summary: Markitdown is a Python tool designed to convert various file and office document formats into Markdown, simplifying content migration and standardization.

github_trending.description: An agentic skills framework & software development methodology that works.
github_trending.summary: The **sponsors/obra** repository provides an agentic skills framework and a practical software development methodology designed to be effective.

github_trending.description: Original Apollo 11 Guidance Computer (AGC) source code for the command and lunar modules.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides the original, historical source code for the Apollo 11 Guidance Computer (AGC) used in both the command and lunar modules.
This repository likely outlines the structure, strategies, and tools used by an AI-driven hedge fund team, focusing on automated trading and quantitative analysis.

github_trending.description: Kronos: A Foundation Model for the Language of Financial Markets
github_trending.summary: Kronos is a foundation model specifically designed to understand and process the language of financial markets, enabling advanced analysis and insights within that domain.

github_trending.description: The agent that grows with you
github_trending.summary: Hermes Agent is a modular, extensible framework designed to facilitate the development and deployment of AI agents that continuously learn and improve over time.

github_trending.description: A collection of notebooks/recipes showcasing some fun and effective ways of using Claude.
github_trending.summary: The `anthropics/claude-cookbooks` repository offers a collection of practical notebooks and recipes demonstrating fun and effective methods for utilizing the Claude AI model.

github_trending.description: from vibe coding to agentic engineering - practice makes claude perfect
github_trending.summary: This repository provides code examples and best practices for leveraging Claude AI, transitioning from basic coding to advanced agentic engineering workflows.
